title: Execute pi worker runtime refactor plan task-by-task
notes: Completed Tasks 1-6 of docs/plans/2026-04-16-pi-worker-runtime-refactor-plan.md plus follow-up hardening for Telegram final-answer relay. Verified npm run check, npm run test:vm, bun test scripts/vm/lib/*.test.ts scripts/vm/lib/backends/*.test.ts, real VM direct runner checks for prompt edge cases, and live gateway/bot restart behavior. Remaining planned major work still starts at Task 7 supervisor Bun core. Before continuing, current branch is being reviewed and packaged for merge to main as stable intermediate milestone.

title: Analyze SmolVM architecture and document integration spike findings
notes: Cloned external/SmolVM, inspected runtime/storage/network/image/browser/dashboard internals, and wrote docs/plans/2026-04-19-smolvm-architecture-analysis.md with diagrams, fit analysis, and recommended spike path.

title: Run isolated SmolVM spike to boot sandbox and try Pi inside it
notes: Extended SmolVM spike. Determined guest pi -p hang was transport-related: over SmolVM/Paramiko it hangs unless stdin is explicitly closed. `pi -p ... </dev/null` exits quickly and reveals real provider/auth error (`No API key found for unknown`). Documented practical practices: use larger disks, explicit CLI delete cleanup, treat QEMU workspace mounts as experimental, and close stdin for non-interactive Pi over SSH.

title: Prove guest-native Pi bootstrap path in SmolVM without copied host settings
notes: Proved guest-native minimal Pi bootstrap in SmolVM without copied host settings: minimal settings.json + copied auth.json was enough for one-shot Pi prompts. Verified Pi can read/edit files and modify a tiny git repo inside guest-local workspace. Explicit smolvm delete cleanup left zero running VMs.

title: Design SmolVM-backed Telegram bot spike using guest-local Pi runtime
notes: Wrote approved design doc `docs/plans/2026-04-20-smolvm-telegram-spike-design.md` and implementation plan `docs/plans/2026-04-20-smolvm-telegram-spike-implementation-plan.md` for separate-token Telegram polling bot that reuses current flow but runs Pi through a SmolVM guest-local backend with stdin-closed non-interactive execution.

title: Implement SmolVM-backed Telegram bot spike backend
notes: Implemented selectable backend registry, SmolVM helper/backend, isolated SmolVM Telegram wrapper, docs, and tests. Verified local unit tests, syntax checks, and `external/SmolVM/.venv/bin/smolvm doctor --backend qemu`. Remaining step is live Telegram round-trip with new bot token/chat allowlist.
